summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with prayer and addressed the common human tendency to wear 'masks'—false fronts that hide our struggles, fears, and imperfections. He explained that these masks are a form of hypocrisy, a term Jesus used repeatedly in Matthew 23 to describe those who look good on the outside but are a mess within. The core of the message focused on dismantling the 'spirit of perfectionism,' which is deeply rooted in fear and insecurity. Pastor Travis outlined four key problems with this mindset: a need for control, a critical and judgmental heart, being a people-pleaser, and relying on artificial self-esteem. He taught that our need for control is ultimately a trust issue, and we must seek God's kingdom first, as taught in Matthew 6. He warned against judging others, reminding us that only God is the qualified judge. The sermon challenged listeners to stop living for the approval of others and to become 'God-pleasers,' finding their true identity, confidence, and fulfillment solely in Jesus Christ. He concluded by encouraging everyone to remove their masks, be authentic before God, and trust in Jehovah Jireh, our provider, who is more than enough.
mainPoints: Perfectionism, driven by fear and a need for control, leads us to wear masks and prevents authentic living.
Judging others and living to please people are traps that reveal our own insecurities and distract us from pleasing God.
True confidence and self-esteem come only from being rooted in Christ, our provider, and not from worldly approval or accomplishments.

versesMentioned.text: Rather, we have renounced secret and shameful ways; we do not use deception, nor do we distort the word of God. On the contrary, by setting forth the truth plainly we commend ourselves to everyone’s conscience in the sight of God.
There is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed, or hidden that will not be made known.

Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith? So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’ For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them. But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
Those who trust in themselves are fools, but those who walk in wisdom are kept safe.
Trust in the LORD with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.

Do not judge, or you too will be judged. For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you. Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye? How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when all the time there is a plank in your own eye? You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
Fear of man will prove to be a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD is kept safe.
On the contrary, we speak as those approved by God to be entrusted with the gospel. We are not trying to please people but God, who tests our hearts.
Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God? Or am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified because of them, for the LORD your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you.

Monday.scripture: Matthew 6:33 (NIV) - But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
Monday.focus: Trusting God with your need for control.
Monday.action: Identify one situation you are trying to control today and consciously pray, 'God, I trust you with this.'
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to seek Your kingdom first today and release my grip on the things I cannot control.

Tuesday.scripture: Matthew 7:1-2 (NIV) - Do not judge, or you too will be judged. For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged...
Tuesday.focus: Replacing a judgmental heart with grace.
Tuesday.action: When you catch yourself making a critical assumption about someone today, stop and pray a blessing for them instead.
Tuesday.prayer: Father, forgive my critical spirit. Help me to see others with Your eyes of grace and love.

Wednesday.scripture: Galatians 1:10 (NIV) - Am I now trying to win the approval of human beings, or of God?... If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
Wednesday.action: Make one decision today based on what you believe honors God, not on what you think will make others happy.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, my desire is to please You alone. Give me the courage to live for an audience of One.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 139:14 (NIV) - I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made; your works are wonderful, I know that full well.
Thursday.focus: Rooting your identity in God's design, not worldly approval.
Thursday.action: Write down one truth from Scripture about who God says you are and reflect on it throughout the day.
Thursday.prayer: God, thank You that I am Your masterpiece. Help my confidence to be found in Your love for me, not in likes or looks.

Friday.scripture: Proverbs 29:25 (NIV) - Fear of man will prove to be a snare, but whoever trusts in the LORD is kept safe.
Friday.focus: Finding security in God's protection, not others' opinions.
Friday.action: Decline one opportunity to 'perform' or seek validation on social media or in conversation today.
Friday.prayer: Jehovah Jireh, You are my provider and protector. I trust in You to keep me safe from the snare of fearing what people think.

Saturday.scripture: Deuteronomy 31:6 (NIV) - Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid or terrified... for the LORD your God goes with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you.
Saturday.focus: Living with courage by removing your mask.
Saturday.action: Have an authentic, non-performative conversation with a trusted friend or family member. Be honest about a struggle.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, give me strength and courage to be real. Thank You for never leaving me. Help me to take off the mask and live freely in Your presence.
